2606.24535.title: Governed Shared Memory for Multi-Agent LLM Systems
2606.24535.paper_summary: Multi-agent LLM environments require robust mechanisms for shared knowledge management. This paper formalizes the fleet-memory problem and identifies four foundational failure modes: unauthorized leakage, stale propagation, contradiction persistence, and provenance collapse. To address these, we define explicit systems-level primitives: scoped retrieval, temporal supersession, provenance tracking, and policy-governed memory propagation. These primitives are implemented in MemClaw, a production multi-tenant memory service, and evaluated via ArgusFleet, a reproducible harness testing four governance dimensions. Rather than a baseline comparison, this study measures a live production service, emphasizing real-world architectural insights and negative results. Key Evaluation Results Provenance: Successfully reconstructed 100% of depth-four derivation chains with correct writer identity at sub-second per-hop latency. Propagation: Demonstrated high intra-fleet visibility with zero cross-fleet leakage. Under strong write mode, write-to-visible latency was optimized to a single search round-trip. Production Architectural Issues Discovered Asymmetric Scope Enforcement: Tenant isolation held, but sub-tenant scope was initially bypassed on direct GET-by-id requests for agent-scoped credentials (disclosed and remediated during the study). Pipeline Ordering Conflict: While contradiction supersession works for admitted writes, a synchronous near-duplicate gate can prematurely reject contradictory writes before the asynchronous contradiction detector can evaluate them. Conclusion: Long-context retrieval alone is insufficient for production multi-agent memory. Governed shared memory demands explicit systems-level abstractions, and live evaluation is vital to expose enforcement and pipeline-ordering failures missed by design-only treatments.
2606.24535.paper_summary_zh: 多代理 LLM 環境需要強健的共享知識管理機制。本文形式化了艦隊記憶問題，並識別出四種基本失效模式：未經授權的洩漏、過時的傳播、矛盾的持續性和來源崩潰。為了解決這些問題，我們定義了明確的系統級原語：範疇檢索、時間超越、來源追蹤和政策治理的記憶傳播。這些原語在 MemClaw 中實現，這是一個生產多租戶記憶服務，並通過 ArgusFleet 進行評估，這是一個可重複的測試四個治理維度的工具。這項研究不是基準比較，而是測量一個實時生產服務，強調現實世界的架構見解和負面結果。關鍵評估結果 來源：成功重建了 100% 的深度四導出鏈，並在每跳延遲低於一秒的情況下正確識別了寫入者身份。傳播：顯示出高內艦隊可見性，且零跨艦隊洩漏。在強寫入模式下，寫入到可見的延遲被優化為單次搜索往返。生產架構問題發現 非對稱範疇執行：租戶隔離保持有效，但在直接的按 ID 獲取請求中，子租戶範疇最初被繞過（在研究期間披露並修正）。管道排序衝突：雖然矛盾超越對已接受的寫入有效，但同步近重複閘門可能會在異步矛盾檢測器能評估之前，過早拒絕矛盾寫入。結論：僅依賴長上下文檢索對於生產多代理記憶是不夠的。治理的共享記憶需要明確的系統級抽象，而實時評估對於揭示設計僅處理中未能發現的執行和管道排序失敗至關重要。
2606.24535.author: Yanki Margalit et.al.
2606.24535.authors: Yanki Margalit, Nurit Cohen-Inger, Erni Avram, Ran Taig, Oded Margalit
